hint: The sky and thunder god in ancient Greek religion.
The hammer-wielding Norse god associated with thunder and lightning.
The ancient Greek god of the underworld.
The Greek god of courage and war.
The widely revered god in Germanic mythology, associated with wisdom and war.
The trickster god in Norse mythology.
Mortals of divine ancestry in mythology, noted for their courage.
A supernatural creature of folk tales, typically represented as a small, elusive figure.
A small imaginary being of human form that has magical powers.
A mythical, cave-dwelling being depicted in folklore as either a giant or a dwarf.
Any of the older gods who preceded the Olympians.
A mythological spirit of nature imagined as a beautiful maiden.
A many-headed serpent or monster in Greek mythology.
The Greek and Roman god of archery, music, and poetry.
The ancient Greek goddess associated with wisdom, handicraft, and warfare.
A mythical monster like a giant reptile, frequently capable of breathing fire.
A Gorgon with living venomous snakes in place of hair.
A mythical winged divine horse.
A fire-breathing female monster with a lion's head, a goat's body, and a serpent's tail.
A mythical creature with the head and wings of an eagle and the body of a lion.
A member of a race of savage one-eyed giants.
A mythological creature with the upper body of a human and the lower body of a horse.
A fictitious or mythical half-human sea creature with the head and trunk of a woman and the tail of a fish.
A mythical animal typically represented as a horse with a single straight horn.
A mythical being that subsists by feeding on the life essence (generally in the form of blood) of the living.
A mischievous, ugly, dwarflike creature of folklore.
An enormous mythical sea monster said to appear off the coasts of Norway.
The Egyptian god of the afterlife, the underworld, and rebirth.
The ancient Greek god of the sea, storms, and earthquakes.
A mythical creature with the head of a bull and the body of a man.
The ancient Greek goddess of sexual love and beauty.
A mischievous sprite in Irish folklore, typically depicted as a little bearded man.
Any of the female figures who choose those who may die in battle and those who may live.
A Titan god of fire who is credited with the creation of humanity from clay.
The legendary sword of King Arthur, sometimes attributed with magical powers.
An immense and central sacred tree in Norse cosmology.
A series of events, including a great battle, foretold to lead to the death of a number of great figures in Norse mythology.
A human with the ability to shapeshift into a wolf.
A mythical sea serpent noted in theology and mythology.
